jokes.text: Boy, that thing's big. / Fry, is my colossal eye too big? / No, it's what makes you you. / But it's so round, so hideously round.
jokes.setup: Leela's insecurity about her appearance
jokes.punchline: Her focus on the roundness being 'hideous' is absurd
jokes.explanation: Good character-specific humor about Leela's cyclops insecurity, well-integrated

jokes.text: Leela, listen, you're the most beautiful woman I've ever known. / Oh, so you only care about my looks? / No, I... what?
jokes.setup: Fry tries to compliment Leela's beauty
jokes.punchline: She immediately twists it into an insult about only caring about looks
jokes.explanation: Classic relationship comedy - the no-win scenario for compliments

jokes.text: I am just a humble emperor with a big, big crown.
jokes.setup: Character introducing himself
jokes.punchline: The contradiction between 'humble' and 'emperor with big crown'
jokes.explanation: Economical oxymoron that establishes character's obliviousness

jokes.text: Of course I'm not planning to steal that crown. / What? / True, I've always dreamt of being an emperor, You know, for Halloween, and then forever
jokes.setup: Bender denying he wants to steal the crown
jokes.punchline: Immediately admits his emperor dreams, escalating from Halloween to forever
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender characterization - the escalation from Halloween to forever is brilliantly written

jokes.text: And finally, a chump To be caught on video and later arrested. / As I told you on sucker-punch day, I'm through being a chump.
jokes.setup: Bender outlining heist roles, needs a chump
jokes.punchline: Fry references 'sucker-punch day' as if it's a real holiday
jokes.explanation: 'Sucker-punch day' as a calendar holiday is absurdist gold and perfectly in character

jokes.text: You see, I own a watch.
jokes.setup: Bender claiming he can provide 'split-second, robotic timing'
jokes.punchline: His qualification is simply owning a watch
jokes.explanation: Perfect comedic economy - the absurd logic delivered with complete confidence

jokes.text: Good, I'm sick of cleaning up those heaps of dead monkeys.
jokes.setup: Professor claims he fixed the mind-switcher
jokes.punchline: Casual mention of dead monkey cleanup as household chore
jokes.explanation: Dark humor made casual - the mundane tone makes the horrific funnier

jokes.text: Like the heaps of dead monkeys? / Science cannot move forward without heaps.
jokes.setup: Amy asking if dead monkeys were Professor's mistake
jokes.punchline: Professor's matter-of-fact justification using 'heaps'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback that doubles down on the absurdity with scientific justification

jokes.text: I wasted my youth porking out. / Yes, I remember. / Would you please take that down? It's making me hungry.
jokes.setup: Amy talking about wasting youth eating, Professor remembers
jokes.punchline: There's apparently a photo/reminder that makes Amy hungry
jokes.explanation: Good character moment for Amy's food obsession, visual gag works

jokes.text: we're just the people This mind-switcher was made for by us.
jokes.setup: Professor realizing he and Amy are perfect candidates
jokes.punchline: Mangled grammar creates circular reasoning
jokes.explanation: Mild wordplay joke, fits Professor's speech patterns but not particularly strong

jokes.text: It didn't work, you doddering old... / Ah, cartilage.
jokes.setup: Amy about to insult the Professor
jokes.punchline: Professor's satisfied reaction to feeling young joints
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection - Amy's anger vs Professor's joy, 'cartilage' is perfectly chosen word

jokes.text: Dude, I bid you a fond cowabunga.
jokes.setup: Professor in Amy's body about to leave
jokes.punchline: Mixing formal farewell structure with 'cowabunga'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant linguistic mash-up that's both quotable and character-appropriate

jokes.text: Yee-haw! Look out, world, here I... [crash sound implied]
jokes.setup: Professor enthusiastically rushing off to live dangerously
jokes.punchline: Immediately crashes, proving Amy's body limitations
jokes.explanation: Classic setup/payoff with good timing, physical comedy that serves the plot

jokes.text: I'm sorry, Amy, but your body's not up to the task. Also, I don't care for these boobs Flapping every which way.
jokes.setup: Professor apologizing for the crash
jokes.punchline: Uncomfortable complaint about experiencing breasts
jokes.explanation: Awkward body-switching humor, characteristic of Professor's blunt observations

jokes.text: You idiot! Once two bodies have switched minds, They can never switch back.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to explain the problem
jokes.punchline: Calling himself an idiot while delivering crucial plot information
jokes.explanation: Self-deprecating humor that advances plot, fits Professor's character

jokes.text: However, perhaps they can.
jokes.setup: Just stated they can never switch back
jokes.punchline: Immediately contradicts himself casually
jokes.explanation: Good contradiction timing, typical Professor flip-flopping on scientific facts

jokes.text: I'm 60% storage space.
jokes.setup: Professor mentions needing temporary storage space
jokes.punchline: Bender's robotic percentage-based self-description
jokes.explanation: Classic Bender percentage joke, perfectly timed and character-specific

jokes.text: I'm afraid we need to use... Math.
jokes.setup: Complex body-switching problem needs solving
jokes.punchline: Treating math like a terrifying last resort
jokes.explanation: Meta-joke about the show's complexity, delivery makes math sound ominous

jokes.text: Stupid air-needing lungs.
jokes.setup: Bender in scuba gear, presumably underwater
jokes.punchline: Complaining about basic biological necessity
jokes.explanation: Perfect Bender complaint - resenting basic human biology while in human body

jokes.text: Four seconds?! / Who goes there? / Three, two, one. / Hello, big boy.
jokes.setup: Bender panicking about having only four seconds to seduce
jokes.punchline: Counts down then delivers cheesy pickup line
jokes.explanation: Good timing gag with the countdown, Bender's desperation comedy works

jokes.text: Silence. I am basil, The emperor's cousin and chief of his royal guard, And I shall not be seduced by a... / I said...
jokes.setup: Guard declaring he won't be seduced
jokes.punchline: Trailing off mid-sentence, implying immediate seduction
jokes.explanation: Classic 'famous last words' setup, the trailing off sells the joke

jokes.text: I hate paying $14 to see Nicolas cage solve things.
jokes.setup: Complaining about senior movie discounts
jokes.punchline: Reducing Nicolas Cage's filmography to 'solving things'
jokes.explanation: Brilliant distillation of Nicolas Cage movies - highly quotable and original observation

jokes.text: see, you do only like me for my body.
jokes.setup: Earlier argument about Fry caring about looks
jokes.punchline: Leela proving her point about Fry's shallowness
jokes.explanation: Good callback that advances the relationship subplot with logical character beats

jokes.text: That is entirely not true, sir.
jokes.setup: Leela accusing Fry of being shallow
jokes.punchline: Overly formal denial that sounds unconvincing
jokes.explanation: Fry's awkward formality when caught makes the denial funnier

jokes.text: Ten minutes ago?!
jokes.setup: Bender realizes he needed to be somewhere much earlier
jokes.punchline: The extreme lateness for his heist timing
jokes.explanation: Good timing comedy, Bender's panic over his failed precision

jokes.text: Uh, could you come back when my chump gets here?
jokes.setup: Bender caught in the middle of his heist
jokes.punchline: Casually asking the victim to reschedule the robbery
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly casual request - treating robbery like a business appointment is perfect Bender

jokes.text: A reverse Turing test, eh? Very good. What is the square root of nine? / Uh, hold on, let me just get out a pencil.
jokes.setup: Testing if Bender is really a robot with math
jokes.punchline: Robot needing pencil for simple arithmetic
jokes.explanation: Great subversion of robot stereotypes - even simple math requires tools

jokes.text: okay, look, I'm not that kind of robot. I'm more this kind of robot. [steps forwards but moves backwards]
jokes.setup: Bender trying to prove he's a robot
jokes.punchline: Demonstrates by malfunctioning in a basic way
jokes.explanation: Good physical gag that fits Bender's character - proving robotness through malfunction

jokes.text: He steps forwards but moves backwards. By the gods, he is a machine.
jokes.setup: Witnessing Bender's malfunction
jokes.punchline: Treating malfunction as proof of mechanical nature
jokes.explanation: Good follow-up that sells the physical gag with royal pompousness

jokes.text: Look, I paid for 15 robot clowns per cubic meter of car, And you're barely giving me 12.
jokes.setup: Professor complaining about some service
jokes.punchline: Specific measurement of clown density in a vehicle
jokes.explanation: Perfectly absurd complaint with scientific precision - 'per cubic meter' makes it brilliantly ridiculous

jokes.text: I am Nonchalanto the robot daredevil, And my dream is to risk my life In strange and original ways.
jokes.setup: Professor introducing his daredevil persona
jokes.punchline: 'Nonchalanto' contradicts the daredevil concept
jokes.explanation: Brilliant name choice - 'Nonchalanto' perfectly captures the Professor's casual approach to danger

jokes.text: That's the dumbest thing anybody's ever done. You're hired.
jokes.setup: Calling something the dumbest ever
jokes.punchline: Immediately hiring based on that assessment
jokes.explanation: Perfect setup/payoff structure - stupidity as qualification is great misdirection

jokes.text: Oh, crap, that is what I am.
jokes.setup: Nikolai describing Bender as common robot with human roommate
jokes.punchline: Bender's deflated self-realization
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Bender's rare moment of honest self-assessment

jokes.text: Hello. / Ah, my fiancée princess Flavia. / Dearest, meet bender. He's a robot in the body of a Chinese Martian physicist. / How tedious.
jokes.setup: Introduction of robot in physicist's body
jokes.punchline: Princess finding this situation 'tedious'
jokes.explanation: Perfect aristocratic dismissal of the bizarre - treating body-switching as boring social news

jokes.text: Can I trust you to do it? / You can trust anything.
jokes.setup: Asking if Bender can be trusted
jokes.punchline: Non-committal response that sounds reassuring
jokes.explanation: Brilliant evasion - sounds like 'trust me' but actually commits to nothing

jokes.text: A man can give her a toke of her own medicine.
jokes.setup: Giving relationship advice
jokes.punchline: 'Toke' instead of 'taste' of her own medicine
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg malapropism - 'toke' changes the meaning entirely

jokes.text: Friends, friends, I've got barnacles on my tuchus.
jokes.setup: Zoidberg making an announcement
jokes.punchline: Oversharing about embarrassing sea-creature problem
jokes.explanation: Perfect Zoidberg overshare - 'tuchus' makes it funnier than just 'butt'

jokes.text: Washbucket full. Scruffy's work here is done.
jokes.setup: Scruffy completing a janitorial task
jokes.punchline: Treating mundane work with heroic completion language
jokes.explanation: Perfect Scruffy line - treating janitor work with comic book hero finality

jokes.text: Switch bodies? I don't see why not. I also don't see why.
jokes.setup: Bender asking to switch bodies
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's complete indifference to bizarre request
jokes.explanation: Quintessential Scruffy - equal apathy toward both sides of an insane proposition

jokes.text: If you're a bender, why is your body full of fetid water? / If you're an emperor, why don't you shut up?!
jokes.setup: Fry asking logical question about body-switching
jokes.punchline: Bender's aggressive non-answer
jokes.explanation: Good Bender deflection - answering question with question plus insult

jokes.text: Robot? / Yes, 'tis I... Bender. Do you not recognize your own best friend? / Aha! The guy in this body has a friend!
jokes.setup: Nikolai trying to convince Fry he's Bender
jokes.punchline: Accidentally reveals he's learning about Bender's friendships
jokes.explanation: Good reveal of the deception through Nikolai's excitement about having friends

jokes.text: But then Nicolas cage discovered The real treasure was his family. / Yes, I'll hold.
jokes.setup: Describing movie plot
jokes.punchline: Reveals she's on hold, describing movie to pass time
jokes.explanation: Nice callback to earlier Cage joke, situational comedy works well

jokes.text: Amy?! Stop it! She's turning me into a parade float. / Ew, look at her!
jokes.setup: Amy forcing Leela to eat
jokes.punchline: Leela becoming comically oversized, Fry's disgusted reaction
jokes.explanation: 'Parade float' is a good visual metaphor for extreme overeating

jokes.text: That wasn't a little extra weight.
jokes.setup: Leela saying Fry is disgusted by 'a little extra weight'
jokes.punchline: Fry's understated correction about the extreme situation
jokes.explanation: Good understated response to the visual gag, Fry being reasonable for once

jokes.text: Sweet orca of Majorca. You make fat Albert look like normal Albert.
jokes.setup: Hermes seeing Leela's enlarged state
jokes.punchline: Creative comparison using Fat Albert reference
jokes.explanation: Brilliant escalation - 'normal Albert' is perfectly absurd follow-up to Fat Albert

jokes.text: Three decades of the munchies beat you to it.
jokes.setup: Amy worried about ruining Hermes' body
jokes.punchline: Hermes admitting to long-term marijuana-induced eating
jokes.explanation: Great self-deprecating humor that reveals character backstory economically

jokes.text: Oh, excuse me. Too much goulash.
jokes.setup: Cannon making bodily function sounds
jokes.punchline: Politely excusing mechanical sounds as digestive issues
jokes.explanation: Good anthropomorphization of machinery with polite social convention

jokes.text: Never! My body may be old, but it is mine, And every crack, a memory of heavy things shot a long way.
jokes.setup: Refusing body switching offer
jokes.punchline: Poetic description of cannon wear as precious memories
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - treating mechanical wear as cherished battle scars

jokes.text: Nikolai, you imperial fool. We've been having an affair for 700 years.
jokes.setup: Basil revealing affair to emperor
jokes.punchline: The impossibly long duration
jokes.explanation: 700 years makes the affair revelation absurdly long-term for robots

jokes.text: You mean, like my penis?
jokes.setup: Fry saying Leela's infirmities make her special
jokes.punchline: Zoidberg's inappropriate anatomical reference
jokes.explanation: Classic Zoidberg inappropriate interjection, completely derails romantic moment

jokes.text: Well, bender, here's our apartment That we live in every day. / A floor? We live like kings!
jokes.setup: Fry showing their modest apartment
jokes.punchline: Nikolai amazed by having a floor
jokes.explanation: Perfect class reversal - emperor thinks basic floor is luxury living

jokes.text: Kings? Silence! Like you, I'm a common lout.
jokes.setup: Trying to blend in as commoner
jokes.punchline: Commanding silence while claiming to be common
jokes.explanation: Good character comedy - can't help being imperial while pretending to be humble

jokes.text: Miss Wong? / No, scruffy, I am washbucket. I love you. Washbucket has always loved you.
jokes.setup: Scruffy addressing washbucket
jokes.punchline: Washbucket declaring eternal love
jokes.explanation: Brilliantly absurd romance between janitor and cleaning equipment with genuine emotion

jokes.text: This is wrong, washbucket. Oh, it would be sweet for a while, But in the back of our minds, we'd know That I'm a man, and you're janitorial equipment.
jokes.setup: Washbucket confessing love
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's practical concerns about human-equipment romance
jokes.explanation: Perfect absurdist romance - treating impossible love with genuine relationship concerns

jokes.text: In another city, we could be anyone we want!
jokes.setup: Scruffy explaining why their love can't work
jokes.punchline: Washbucket's desperate romantic plea
jokes.explanation: Classic desperate romance trope played completely straight between man and bucket

jokes.text: Go. Go now. Before I beg you to stay.
jokes.setup: Washbucket's romantic plea
jokes.punchline: Scruffy's melodramatic rejection
jokes.explanation: Perfect melodramatic romance dialogue delivered to janitorial equipment

jokes.text: Shut up, madam ambassador. I know it.
jokes.setup: Ambassador expressing love for emperor
jokes.punchline: Bender rudely dismissing diplomatic courtesy
jokes.explanation: Good Bender rudeness in formal diplomatic setting

jokes.text: I'd now like to digress from my prepared remarks To say I'm done.
jokes.setup: Bender giving UN speech
jokes.punchline: Formal announcement of giving up immediately
jokes.explanation: Perfect diplomatic language to announce complete laziness - brilliantly formal

jokes.text: You're robo-Hungarian? / Yes. Our nation's chief export is carnies.
jokes.setup: Learning about character's nationality
jokes.punchline: Country's main export being carnival workers
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist worldbuilding - treating carnies as natural resource

jokes.text: I have not so much time left anyhow. But you could get a new body. You could have a rich, full life! / I am trying to have a rich, full life.
jokes.setup: Professor trying to convince Bertha to get new body
jokes.punchline: Bertha's dying act IS her rich, full life
jokes.explanation: Beautiful character moment - choosing heroic death over safe life extension

jokes.text: That is not what I meant to give you for big Bertha.
jokes.setup: Professor apparently attacking with wrong weapon/gesture
jokes.punchline: Understated admission of tactical error
jokes.explanation: Good understatement during action sequence, Professor's matter-of-fact correction

jokes.text: Good, 'cause I've got 20 of them per cubic meter.
jokes.setup: Villain saying he likes killing brave things
jokes.punchline: Reference to earlier clown density measurement
jokes.explanation: Excellent callback to earlier absurd measurement, works in action context

jokes.text: When I grow up, I want to be a diplomat!
jokes.setup: Child witnessing violent UN confrontation
jokes.punchline: Being inspired by diplomatic violence
jokes.explanation: Good dark humor about children's impressions of adult violence

jokes.text: Q to the e to the d.
jokes.setup: Mathematical solution being presented
jokes.punchline: Hermes' mathematical expression of 'QED'
jokes.explanation: Clever mathematical wordplay fitting Hermes' bureaucratic precision

jokes.text: And they say pure math has no real-world applications!
jokes.setup: Complex math solving body-switching problem
jokes.punchline: Defending abstract mathematics with absurd application
jokes.explanation: Good meta-joke about the episode's mathematical complexity and academic utility

jokes.text: You'll win the NBA's highest academic honor for this!
jokes.setup: Praising mathematical achievement
jokes.punchline: Confusing NBA with academic institution
jokes.explanation: Good Professor confusion between sports league and academic honors

jokes.text: And I'm left with the real jewels safely inside... His compartment?! Oh!
jokes.setup: Bender thinking he successfully stole jewels
jokes.punchline: Realizing they're in the wrong body compartment
jokes.explanation: Good setup/payoff for the heist failure, body-switching consequences
